name: Red bell pepper (food medium)

components.name: water
components.evidence: Matrix is ~92% water; dilute aqueous medium.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Free monosaccharide; co-dominant sugar in ripe red pepper.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Free monosaccharide; dominant sugar. Glucose+fructose ~= total sugars (4.2 g), i.e. red pepper sugar is almost entirely hexose monosaccharides.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Minor and variable; sucrose falls during ripening so ripe RED fruit is low in sucrose (green fruit has more). Value approximate; near-trace.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: HPLC pepper study PMC9871545 (2023): citric 1.82-5.60 mg/g FW, 24.6-49.5% of total organic acids; rises during ripening
components.evidence: DOMINANT non-ascorbate organic acid of pepper. Value is a red-appropriate midpoint of the 1.82-5.60 mg/g (182-560 mg/100g) reported range.

components.name: L-ascorbic acid (vitamin C)
components.source: USDA FDC 170108; corroborated PMC7346132 (0.82-1.20 mg/g in red varieties)
components.evidence: Quantitatively a major organic acid in red pepper, not just a vitamin; increases with ripening. Listed once here (also a vitamin).

components.name: L-malic acid
components.source: HPLC pepper study PMC9871545: malic 0.99-1.88 mg/g FW, 12.7-21.6% of total; decreases during ripening
components.evidence: Second/third most abundant organic acid; red-appropriate midpoint of 99-188 mg/100g range.

components.name: fumaric acid
components.source: HPLC pepper study PMC9871545: fumaric 1.48-3.71 mg/g FW (18.8-33.8% of total)
components.evidence: Reported as abundant in that dataset (unusually high vs most matrices); include with caveat that other studies find fumaric only trace. Treat value as uncertain.

components.name: alpha-ketoglutaric acid
components.source: HPLC pepper study PMC9871545: 0.70-1.43 mg/g FW (10.4-16.8% of total)
components.evidence: Detected organic acid / TCA intermediate; midpoint of reported range.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: HPLC pepper study PMC9871545: <0.7 mg/g FW (<8% of total)

components.name: protein (total)
components.evidence: Total crude protein; amino-acid breakdown below.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Co-most-abundant; major free amino acid / flavour.

components.name: L-proline
components.evidence: Free proline accumulates in ripe pepper.

components.name: beta-carotene (provitamin A)
components.evidence: 157 ug RAE; red pepper is carotenoid-rich.

remove.name: Silver
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant metal, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Rubidium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace element, not a growth nutrient (uncovered list)

remove.name: Chromium
remove.reason: trace element reported at contaminant level; not a defined pepper nutrient

remove.name: Creatine cytosol
remove.reason: animal muscle metabolite; biologically implausible in a plant fruit; FooDB predicted

remove.name: Creatine
remove.reason: animal metabolite, implausible in plant matrix; FooDB predicted

remove.name: Taurine C2H7NO3S
remove.reason: animal metabolite, not synthesized by plants; FooDB predicted/implausible

remove.name: 17alpha-ethynylestradiol
remove.reason: synthetic contraceptive drug; biologically implausible; FooDB predicted noise (uncovered list)

remove.name: N-Methylaniline
remove.reason: industrial aniline derivative; implausible endogenous pepper compound; FooDB noise

remove.name: Racemethionine
remove.reason: synthetic DL-methionine descriptor duplicating L-methionine; artifact

remove.name: Linoelaidic acid
remove.reason: trans-fat isomer; not a natural pepper lipid; FooDB predicted

missing_added: citric acid (dominant organic acid, absent from current list)
individual sugar amounts (glucose, fructose) with quantities
sucrose noted as near-trace in ripe red fruit
quantitative amino-acid, vitamin, mineral, and fatty-acid panel
keep_uncovered: Capsaicin / Dihydrocapsaicin / Nordihydrocapsaicin / Homocapsaicin (capsaicinoids: essentially ABSENT in sweet bell pepper - non-pungent cultivar - so present only at trace; genuine to genus but ~0 here)
Capsianosides A-IV, Capsicosides A1/B1/C1 (genuine pepper acyclic diterpene/steroidal glycosides)
p-Coumaric acid, Caffeic acid, Luteolin (genuine phenolics/flavonoids)
Nonivamide, Moupinamide, Capsiamide, Capsianside A/B (genuine capsaicinoid-related amides)
Boron, Silicon (genuine plant micronutrients, trace; no BiGG metabolite)
Beta-carotene, beta-cryptoxanthin, lutein, zeaxanthin, violaxanthin, lycopene, alpha-carotene (genuine carotenoids)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 170108 (Peppers, sweet, red, raw)
PMC9871545 - Comprehensive fruit quality assessment, Capsicum annuum, organic acids by HPLC (2023)
PMC7346132 - Sarno et al. 2020, Biochemical Characterization of Sweet Pepper, Campania (ascorbic acid in red varieties)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources are the free hexoses D-fructose (~2.3 g) and D-glucose (~1.9 g) plus citric acid (~0.3 g); sucrose is near-trace in ripe red fruit. Ascorbic acid (vitamin C, 128 mg/100g) is also a major organic acid. Organic-acid amounts (citric, malic, fumaric, alpha-ketoglutarate, oxalic) are red-appropriate midpoints of HPLC ranges and carry moderate uncertainty; the fumaric value in particular is unusually high in the source study and should be treated as tentative. Capsaicinoids are effectively zero because this is a sweet (non-pungent) bell pepper. Removed FooDB noise: heavy-metal contaminants and animal/synthetic compounds (creatine, taurine, ethynylestradiol).
